message:
fix(cards): UpgradeStatus re-fetches after cluster upgrade (#6292) (#6296)

@xonas1101 reported that after upgrading a cluster, the "Cluster
Upgrade Status" card keeps showing the old Kubernetes version and
the "Upgrade" button remains visible. Verified against the source:

The fetch effect at UpgradeStatus.tsx:442 filters out clusters
already in `fetchedClustersRef.current`:

    const clustersToFetch = reachableClusters.filter(c =>
      !fetchedClustersRef.current.has(c.name) ||
      failedClustersRef.current.has(c.name))

Once a cluster's version is successfully fetched, its name is added
to `fetchedClustersRef` and never removed except on component unmount.
The existing 15s retry interval only runs for FAILED clusters. There
was no mechanism to revisit a cluster whose version had CHANGED
(e.g. after an upgrade mission succeeded).

The file already declares VERSION_CACHE_TTL = 5 * 60 * 1000 at the
top but nothing ever honored the TTL — the persistent `versionCache`
was TTL-aware (getCachedVersion checks age) but the per-mount
`fetchedClustersRef` deduping set was not.

Fix: add a second setInterval on VERSION_CACHE_TTL that clears
`fetchedClustersRef` AND evicts the relevant entries from the
persistent `versionCache`, then re-runs fetchVersions. After an
upgrade, the card now reflects the new version within the TTL
window (5 minutes worst case).

Closes #6292

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
